numberedSections.heading: 1.1 OpenAI-API-key-pair-Wire + GPT-4o-clone-voice-onboard + AI-orchestration-engine-baseline-Wire
numberedSections.body:
Per research/16 Pillar 1 + Pitfall #2 OpenAI-API-prereq.

```
GENERATIVE-AI-ENGINE-ONBOARDING-PACK (decision tree)

For <$1M US-GMV brands (Path A):
  → OpenAI-API-key-pair-Wired + AI-orchestration-engine-baseline-Wired (Pencil Lite $99/mo or AdCreative.ai Standard $249/mo)
  → Best for cash-constrained first-time AI-engine-test; defer full-stack-AI-engine-launch-until-AI-substrate-matures

For $1M-$25M DTC GMV brands (Path B DEFAULT):
  → OpenAI-API-key-pair-Wired + AI-orchestration-engine-baseline-Wired (Pencil Pro $1,500/mo or Moby Pro $499/mo)
  → + Jasper-brand-voice-LLM-Wired ($49-$125/mo)
  → + Copy.ai-Wired ($49-$249/mo)
  → + Triple-Whale-AI-creative-cohort-overlay-Wired (Starter $179/mo or Pro $1,290/mo)
  → + Midjourney-Wired ($30-$120/mo)
  → + ElevenLabs-Wired ($5-$330/mo)
  → + Typeface-brand-voice-Wired ($25-$100/mo)
  → Best for default mid-market AI-engine-launch with full 5-pillar framework

For $5M+ DTC+international GMV brands (Path C enterprise):
  → + OpenAI-fine-tuning-Enterprise-license
  → + custom-trained-LLM-on-brand-voice-data-Enterprise-baseline
  → + Midjourney-Pro $30-$600/mo + Stable-Diffusion-XL-Enterprise $50-$500/mo
  → + Runway-Gen-2 $95-$500/mo + Sora-Enterprise $100-$1k/mo
  → + dedicated-AI-engine-team $4k-$6k/mo
  → + international-multi-locale-AI-engine-Enterprise-rollout
  → Best for enterprise scale with custom-trained-LLM + dedicated-team
```

```bash
# Step 1: OpenAI-API-key-pair on https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
# Expected: sk-... live with GPT-4o + GPT-4o-fine-tuning + DALL-E-3 + TTS-1 + Whisper access
# Cost: Pay-as-you-go, $20-$200/mo typical usage for $1M-$25M Path B brand

# Step 2: AI-orchestration-engine-platform (Pencil Pro or Moby Pro or AdCreative.ai Standard)
# Expected: Pencil Pro $1,500/mo OR Moby Pro $499/mo OR AdCreative.ai Standard $249/mo
# Verified: brand-voice-LLM-pair-Wired + AI-creative-iteration-velocity-tracked at 50+-creatives/week baseline

# Step 3: GPT-4o-clone-voice-onboard
# 1. Wire OpenAI-API-key-pair to AI-orchestration-engine (Pencil Pro or Moby Pro)
# 2. Configure brand-voice-API-key-pair (per OpenAI 2024 + Anthropic 2024 pricing)
# 3. Verify GPT-4o-clone-voice with 5-15% email-CTR-lift test vs Move #10 baseline
# 4. Document the canonical 5-voice × 5-format = 25 voice-format AI-creative-templates

# Step 4: Wire Triple-Whale-AI-creative-cohort-overlay
# 1. Open Triple-Whale-AI-creative-cohort-overlay-Settings page
# 2. Wire OpenAI-API-key + AI-orchestration-engine-key
# 3. Verify AI-creative-cohort-LTV-at-30/60/90/180-day-windows
# 4. Set canonical AI-creative-cohort-overlay-cron: weekly Monday 09:00
```

Expected outcome: OpenAI-API-key-pair-Wired + GPT-4o-clone-voice-onboarded + AI-orchestration-engine-baseline-Wired (Pencil Pro or Moby Pro) + Triple-Whale-AI-creative-cohort-overlay-Wired + canonical 5-voice × 5-format = 25 voice-format AI-creative-templates documented + first-AI-creative-iteration-flow launched. Per AdCreative.ai 2024 + Moby 2024 + Pencil 2024 + Jasper 2024 + Typeface 2024 + Midjourney 2024 + Runway 2024 + Sora 2024 + OpenAI 2024 + Anthropic 2024 + Triple-Whale 2024 benchmarks: GPT-4o-clone-voice-onboarding typically achieves 5-15% email-CTR-lift-vs-AI-baseline; AI-orchestration-engine-onboarding typically achieves +20-40% creative-iteration-velocity-vs-Moby-baseline.

numberedSections.heading: 1.2 Jasper-brand-voice-LLM-Wired + Copy.ai-ad-copy-iteration-Wired + first-AI-creative-iteration-flow-launched
numberedSections.body:
Per research/16 Pillar 1 + Pitfall #3 Jasper-brand-voice-LLM-prereq.

```bash
# Step 1: Jasper-brand-voice-LLM-onboard
# 1. Sign up at https://www.jasper.ai (Creator $49/mo or Teams $125/mo)
# 2. Upload 50-100 brand-content-samples (website + email + social + ad-copy)
# 3. Train Jasper-brand-voice-LLM on 5-voice × 5-format = 25 voice-format-templates
# 4. Verify Jasper-brand-voice-LLM-output vs Move #10 baseline (typically +5-15% email-CTR-lift)
# 5. Wire Jasper-API-key to AI-orchestration-engine (Pencil Pro or Moby Pro)

# Step 2: Copy.ai-ad-copy-iteration-onboard
# 1. Sign up at https://www.copy.ai (Free or Pro $49/mo or Enterprise $249/mo)
# 2. Build ad-copy-iteration baseline (50+ ad-copy-variants-per-week-baseline)
# 3. Wire Copy.ai-API-key to AI-orchestration-engine
# 4. Verify Copy.ai-ad-copy-iteration-output vs Move #10 baseline (typically +5-15% ad-CTR-lift)

# Step 3: Midjourney-product-photography-onboard (Path B+C only)
# 1. Subscribe to Midjourney at https://www.midjourney.com (Basic $10/mo or Standard $30/mo or Pro $60/mo)
# 2. Build Midjourney-prompt-library with 50+-prompts-per-product-category
# 3. Wire Midjourney-API to AI-orchestration-engine
# 4. Verify Midjourney-product-photography vs photographer-baseline (typically 50-70% cost-savings + 2-4× velocity)

# Step 4: ElevenLabs-voice-clone-onboard (Path B+C only)
# 1. Subscribe to ElevenLabs at https://elevenlabs.io (Free or Starter $5/mo or Creator $22/mo or Pro $99/mo or Scale $330/mo)
# 2. Clone brand-voice via 1-3 minute voice-sample
# 3. Wire ElevenLabs-API to AI-orchestration-engine
# 4. Verify ElevenLabs-voice-clone vs voiceover-artist-baseline (typically 50-90% cost-savings)

# Step 5: Typeface-brand-voice-Wire (Path B+C only)
# 1. Subscribe to Typeface at https://www.typeface.ai ($25-$100/mo)
# 2. Upload brand-style-guide + 50-100 brand-content-samples
# 3. Train Typeface-brand-voice on 5-voice × 5-format = 25 voice-format-templates
# 4. Wire Typeface-API to AI-orchestration-engine

# Step 6: First-AI-creative-iteration-flow-launch
# 1. Pick a flagship-product (typically highest-LTV SKU)
# 2. Run AI-ad-creative-iteration-via-Pencil (or Moby)
# 3. Run AI-product-photography-iteration-via-Midjourney
# 4. Run AI-email-subject-line-iteration-via-Jasper
# 5. Verify 5-15% email-CTR-lift + 10-30% ROAS-lift vs Move #10 baseline
# 6. Document the canonical first-AI-creative-iteration-flow-baseline
```

Expected outcome: Jasper-brand-voice-LLM-Wired + Copy.ai-Wired + Midjourney-Wired + ElevenLabs-Wired + Typeface-Wired + first-AI-creative-iteration-flow-launched. Per Jasper 2024 + Copy.ai 2024 + Typeface 2024 + Midjourney 2024 + ElevenLabs 2024 + Smartwriter 2024 + Cohere 2024 + OpenAI 2024 + Anthropic 2024 benchmarks: Jasper-brand-voice-LLM-Wired typically achieves 5-15% email-CTR-lift-vs-AI-baseline; Midjourney-product-photography-iteration typically achieves 50-70% creative-production-cost-savings + 2-4× creative-iteration-velocity-vs-photographer-baseline.

Step 4: Build canonical 5+-product-rec-feed-templates (homepage + PDP + cart + post-purchase + email)
Step 5: Wire to Move #2 post-purchase-upsell + Move #11 subscription-program + Move #14 lifecycle-marketing
Step 6: Verify 5-15% AOV-lift + 10-30% repeat-purchase-rate-lift vs no-AI-baseline

# Expected outcome: AI-customer-service-response-baselined + AI-product-rec-feed-personalized
# Per Gorgias 2024 + Jasper 2024 + Typeface 2024 + Zendesk-AI 2024 + Intercom-Fin-AI 2024 + Nosto 2024 + Rebuy 2024 + Triple-Whale 2024 + Sailthru 2024 + Dynamic-Yield 2024 benchmarks: AI-customer-service-response-baseline typically achieves 30-50% customer-service-cost-savings + 10-30% customer-service-CSAT-lift-vs-no-AI-baseline; AI-product-rec-feed-personalization typically achieves 5-15% AOV-lift + 10-30% repeat-purchase-rate-lift-vs-no-AI-baseline.

assets.title: Goal
meta: 8 templates across 4 flow types: 3 email (welcome #1 / cart-abandon #1 / cart-abandon #2 escalation) + 4 SMS (welcome / cart-abandon #1 / cart-abandon #2 escalation / review request) + 1 win-back email template (60+ days inactive).

Each template is paste-ready: subject line A/B variants, preview text, full body (≤160 chars for SMS), variable map ({{ first_name }}, {{ event.extra.line_items.0.title }}, etc.), and a one-line when to use / when NOT to use rule.
Compliance baked in: CAN-SPAM physical address + unsubscribe footer in every email; TCPA "Reply STOP to opt out" + double-opt-in checkbox + 10DLC registration reminder in every SMS.

Klaviyo + Postscript native variables: every {{ ... }} in the templates resolves from the standard Shopify-integrated event payload. Operator pastes the template into Klaviyo's flow editor, Klaviyo auto-fills the variables per recipient, no manual merge tags required.
Sibling-consistency: every template references the playbook it came from so operators who need the full step-by-step can jump to the playbook in one click.

assets.title: Goal
meta: 5 voice profiles covering the most common brand types in DTC: Default (confident + plain + lightly irreverent) / Luxury (refined + quiet + maximalist grammar) / Sustainable / eco (warm + earnest + educational) / Gen-Z playful (high-energy + lowercase + meme-aware) / B2B / professional (precise + benefit-led + no jokes).

5-dimension voice framework (formality / humor / brand-name usage / price disclosure / objection handling) — every profile is defined as a position on each dimension. Operators can interpolate between profiles (e.g. "luxury + slightly warmer" = refined + earnest) instead of picking from a fixed menu.

Worked adapted Template 1 for each profile — operators see the diff between Default and their target voice in one side-by-side block. After reading, they can adapt any of Asset 01's other 7 templates in 5-15 minutes per template.
Decision matrix at the bottom — operators answer 3-5 questions about their brand and the matrix recommends a profile (Default / Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B).

Common pitfalls list (10 named, each with corrective Fix line) — the mistakes operators make when they skip voice work (using "Dear valued customer" / writing in third person / over-using emojis / undercutting premium pricing with discount-led copy).
Verification gates (5 gates per template after adaptation) — the runnable check that the adapted voice passes a quick smoke test before activating the flow.

assets.title: Goal
meta: Three sourcing models covered end-to-end: (1) Organic reviews (auto-collected via Yotpo / Loox / Judge.me from real customers, triggered by Asset 01's SMS Review Request template + post-purchase Klaviyo email flow) — free, slow (30–90 days to first 50 reviews), high-trust; (2) Gifted creator content (free product in exchange for 1 IG/TikTok post + usage rights) — low-cost ($30 COGS), medium-trust, mid-speed (14–30 days); (3) Paid creator content (cash + free product in exchange for 1–3 posts + usage rights for ads) — high-cost ($150–$500/creator), highest-trust when creator-audience-fit is good, fast (7–14 days).

5 creator outreach email templates paste-ready for Klaviyo (or Gmail): paid initial pitch / gifted initial pitch / affiliate / re-engagement / declining politely (for creators who don't fit). Each template uses the operator's brand voice (see Asset 02) and includes the variables an operator pastes in.

3 contract templates paste-ready for Google Docs or Notion: paid creator agreement / gifted creator agreement / affiliate creator agreement. Each names the deliverable, the usage rights (organic vs paid amplification), the timeline, and the kill-fee clause.

Klaviyo UGC segment tag — a source = ugc-creator property added to the customer's profile when a creator's audience converts, so operators can measure creator-driven LTV vs paid LTV separately (the cohort overlay that Move #9.5 PDP A/B testing + Move #10 AI ad creative both depend on).

Commissioning template (the "creative brief" sent to every creator regardless of paid/gifted/affiliate) — a 1-page Google Doc that names the product, the angle, the deliverables, the do's-and-don'ts, and the usage-rights scope.

Sibling-consistency: every outreach email references Asset 01's SMS Review Request template (organic path), Asset 02's brand voice (outreach tone), Move #9's PDP reviews block (the destination), and Move #9.5's cohort-LTV overlay (the measurement).

numberedSections.heading: 1. Parties
numberedSections.body:
- **Brand:** [BRAND LEGAL NAME], [ADDRESS], [EIN/TAX ID]
- **Creator:** [CREATOR LEGAL NAME OR HANDLE], [ADDRESS], [TAX ID / W-9 on file]

numberedSections.heading: 2. Deliverables
numberedSections.body:
- **Quantity:** [N] posts across [IG feed / Reels / TikTok / Stories]
- **First draft due:** [DATE] (typically 14 days after product receipt)
- **Final approval:** required from Brand before posting
- **Posting window:** [DATE RANGE — typically 14–30 days after final approval]
- **Platform handles to tag:** @[BRAND HANDLE]

numberedSections.heading: 3. Compensation
numberedSections.body:
- **Cash:** $[AMOUNT] USD, paid within [N] days of final approval via [PayPal / Wise / ACH]
- **Product:** 1 free [PRODUCT NAME] (MSRP $[AMOUNT]), shipped within 48h of contract signing
- **Affiliate commission (optional):** [COMMISSION]% of attributed revenue for [N] days from posting

numberedSections.heading: 4. Usage rights
numberedSections.body:
- **Organic repost:** Creator grants Brand non-exclusive right to repost on Brand's owned channels (IG, TikTok, FB, brand website) with @[CREATOR HANDLE] credit for [N] months from posting date
- **Paid amplification:** Creator grants Brand non-exclusive right to use Content in paid ads (Meta, TikTok, Google) for [N] months from posting date
- **Whitelisting:** [Creator grants Brand the right to run Meta Partnership Ads using Creator's handle for [N] months / Brand does NOT have whitelisting rights]
- **Spark Ads (TikTok):** [Creator grants Brand the right to boost via TikTok Spark Ads for [N] months / Brand does NOT have Spark Ads rights]
- **Attribution window:** [30 / 60 / 90] days from posting date for affiliate commission + cohort-LTV overlay

numberedSections.heading: 5. Kill fee (if Brand cancels mid-cycle)
numberedSections.body:
- **After product ships but before first draft:** Brand pays [25–50]% of full cash fee + Creator keeps product
- **After first draft is submitted:** Brand pays [50–75]% of full cash fee + Creator keeps product
- **After final draft is approved:** Brand pays 100% of full cash fee + Creator keeps product
- **After content is posted:** Brand pays 100% of full cash fee + Creator keeps product + Brand retains usage rights as specified in Section 4

numberedSections.heading: 6. Kill fee (if Creator cancels mid-cycle)
numberedSections.body:
- **Before first draft:** Creator returns product (if received) within 7 days at Brand's expense; no further obligations
- **After first draft:** Creator forfeits any unpaid cash fee; Brand retains rights to the draft content (with credit) for [N] months
- **After final draft approval but before posting:** Creator forfeits any unpaid cash fee; Brand retains rights to the approved content (with credit) for [N] months

numberedSections.heading: 7. FTC compliance
numberedSections.body:
- Creator will tag all posts with #ad (or platform-specific paid partnership tag — e.g. "Paid partnership with [BRAND]" on IG) as the first hashtag AND in the first 2 lines of caption
- Creator will use clear disclosure language ("I was paid to create this content" / "Gifted in exchange for this post") if asked by platform or audience
- Creator will not make claims outside the approved brief (Section X of the brief)
- Brand will provide claim language in the brief; Creator is not liable for claims made by Brand

numberedSections.heading: 8. Confidentiality
numberedSections.body:
- Both parties agree to keep compensation terms confidential unless required to disclose by law
- Creator will not disclose unreleased products to third parties before public launch
- Brand will not disclose Creator's private analytics (audience demographics, engagement rates) to third parties without permission

numberedSections.heading: 9. Termination
numberedSections.body:
- Either party may terminate for cause (material breach) with 7 days written notice
- Upon termination, both parties retain rights to content already posted; kill fees in Sections 5–6 apply
- Creator may remove their content from their own handle after the usage-rights window expires (Section 4) with 30 days notice to Brand

numberedSections.heading: 10. Governing law
numberedSections.body:
- This agreement is governed by the laws of [STATE / COUNTRY]
- Disputes will be resolved via [ARBITRATION SERVICE — e.g. American Arbitration Association] in [CITY]

numberedSections.heading: 1. Parties
numberedSections.body:
- **Brand:** [BRAND LEGAL NAME], [ADDRESS]
- **Creator:** [CREATOR HANDLE], [SHIPPING ADDRESS]

numberedSections.heading: 2. Product gift
numberedSections.body:
- **Product:** 1 free [PRODUCT NAME] (MSRP $[AMOUNT])
- **Ships within:** 48h of contract signing
- **Creator keeps product:** yes, regardless of whether content is posted

numberedSections.heading: 3. Deliverables
numberedSections.body:
- **Quantity:** 1 post (IG feed / Reel / TikTok — Creator's choice)
- **First draft due:** [DATE] (typically 14 days after product receipt)
- **Final approval:** optional (Creator may post without Brand approval if using organic-only rights)
- **Posting window:** [DATE RANGE — typically 14–30 days after product receipt]
- **Platform handles to tag:** @[BRAND HANDLE]

numberedSections.heading: 4. Compensation
numberedSections.body:
- **Cash:** $0 (gifted product only)
- **Affiliate commission (optional):** [COMMISSION]% of attributed revenue for [N] days from posting (Creator must enroll in Brand's affiliate program separately)

numberedSections.heading: 5. Usage rights
numberedSections.body:
- **Organic repost:** Creator grants Brand non-exclusive right to repost on Brand's owned channels with @[CREATOR HANDLE] credit for [N] months from posting date
- **Paid amplification:** [Creator grants Brand the right to use Content in paid ads for [N] months / Brand does NOT have paid amplification rights — gifted creators typically decline this; if paid rights are required, use Contract C1 paid instead]
- **Whitelisting / Spark Ads:** [typically NOT granted for gifted creators — discuss separately if needed]

numberedSections.heading: 6. FTC compliance
numberedSections.body:
- Creator will tag all posts with #gifted or #sp (sponsored product) AND #ad (because the product was given in exchange for content)
- Creator will not make claims outside the approved brief (Section X of the brief)

numberedSections.heading: 7. Kill fee
numberedSections.body:
- **If Creator cancels before first draft:** Creator returns product within 7 days at Brand's expense
- **If Creator cancels after posting:** no kill fee (product is already Creator's; Brand retains usage rights as specified in Section 5)

numberedSections.heading: 1. Parties
numberedSections.body:
- **Brand:** [BRAND LEGAL NAME], [ADDRESS]
- **Creator:** [CREATOR LEGAL NAME], [ADDRESS], [TAX ID / W-9 on file]

numberedSections.heading: 2. Referral program
numberedSections.body:
- **Platform:** [ShareASale / Refersion / GoAffPro / Brand-direct]
- **Unique link:** [REFERRAL LINK]
- **Cookie window:** [30 / 60] days from first click
- **Commission rate:** [COMMISSION]% of net order value (after returns + discounts; before shipping + taxes)

numberedSections.heading: 3. Payouts
numberedSections.body:
- **Schedule:** monthly (or upon request if balance exceeds $[THRESHOLD])
- **Method:** [PayPal / Wise / Direct deposit]
- **Minimum payout:** $[THRESHOLD] (typically $50–100); balances below threshold roll over to next month
- **Hold period:** [N] days from order to allow for returns (typically 30–60 days; longer for high-AOV / high-return-rate categories)

assets.title: Goal
meta: 12 monthly slots — one per month — with the voice profile, discount intensity, audience segment, template stack (which Asset 01 templates fire that month), UGC campaign status (creator-driven months get Asset 03 outreach emails), and loyalty program state (member-only slots vs always-on).

Q1-low / Q2-creator / Q3-mid / Q4-peak macro shape — the calendar is anchored on real retail seasonality (Q1 = post-holiday recovery, Q4 = BFCM + holiday peak), not on "ship a promo every month for the sake of shipping a promo" (the discount-fatigue trap from Pitfall #1 below).

Voice profile explicitly chosen per month — Default brands run Default every month (Discount frequency = Monthly per Asset 02's matrix); Luxury brands run Luxury every month (Discount frequency = Never/seasonal — only the 4 seasonal peak slots get a discount, the other 8 months are full-price); Sustainable brands run Sustainable every month with member-only slot gates; Gen-Z brands run Daily/flash sales aligned with launch drops; B2B brands ignore consumer seasonality and run a quarterly-volume cadence (last table in this asset).

Discount intensity calibrated to voice — the same 20%-off holiday slot reads differently in Default vs Luxury vs Sustainable voice (Asset 02 Pitfall #5 + #7 cover the "Luxury voice + '10% off'" trap and the "Sustainable voice + discount-led" trap).

Klaviyo + Postscript template stack — for each month, a named list of which Asset 01 templates fire (e.g. "January fires T1 Welcome + T2 Cart-Abandon Soft + T8 Win-Back; suppress T4-T7 SMS until February").
UGC campaign cadence — Q2 (April–June) + Q4 (October–December) are creator-driven campaign months per Asset 03's recommended cadence; the 5 outreach emails in Asset 03 fire in those months.

assets.title: Goal
meta: 12 named metrics covering acquisition-side retention (CAC / payback / LTV / LTV:CAC), behavior-side retention (retention rate D1/D7/D30/D90 / repeat purchase rate / churn rate / cohort LTV / unsubscribe rate), and brand-side retention (NPS / email engagement) — the full retention-health surface area
Canonical formula + default benchmark range + Klaviyo/Shopify/Triple Whale data source for each metric — paste-ready into a monthly snapshot spreadsheet or dashboard tile
4 voice-driven benchmark overrides (Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B) — same shape as Asset 04's 4 variant tables; operators in a non-default voice apply the override column instead of the Default

Decision matrix that ranks the 12 metrics by ROI-per-hour-of-measurement-effort: ship metrics #1–#3 (CAC + LTV + LTV:CAC) first because they unblock Move #6 Triple Whale's "killer test" + Move #9.5 PDP A/B testing's "cohort overlay check"; ship metrics #4–#6 (retention rate + RPR + churn) next because they measure the moves you're shipping; defer metrics #10–#12 (NPS + email engagement + unsubscribe rate) to month 2+ because they're leading indicators

The 10 most common measurement pitfalls with corrective Fix: lines — including the "Triple Whale vs GA4 mismatch" trap (Move #6 Pitfall #15), the "shipped-template-but-don't-know-which-metric-moved" trap (the central operator question this asset closes), and the "benchmarks don't match my brand voice" trap (the 4 voice-driven overrides are the fix)

5 verification gates — monthly-snapshot-completeness / formula-correctness / benchmark-applicability / data-source-pipeline / trend-direction — that an operator runs before declaring the snapshot valid

assets.title: Goal
meta: 3 survey channels — Postscript SMS (1-question, post-purchase, 14-day delay), Wootric email (1-question + 9 follow-up questions, 30-day delay), Delighted (free tier, web + email, 7-day delay). Each has explicit pricing, response-rate, and use-case fit.

A 1-question + 9-question survey template — the canonical 1-question NPS survey ("How likely are you to recommend {{ company.name }} to a friend?") + the 9-question follow-up that probes detractors vs promoters with verbatim-capture rules.

Survey-frequency guardrails — the canonical "survey-fatigue trap" rules (max 1 NPS survey per customer per 90 days; 3 NPS surveys per year per customer max; no NPS survey within 14 days of a complaint or refund).
Klaviyo NPS segment wiring — 4-step recipe to set nps_score custom property + tag promoters (9–10) vs passives (7–8) vs detractors (0–6) + feed cohort-by-NPS-bucket into the cohort-LTV overlay.

Cohort-by-NPS-bucket LTV analysis — the canonical query to compute 90-day / 180-day / 365-day LTV by NPS bucket (Promoter LTV / Passive LTV / Detractor LTV), which proves the Move #8 loyalty + Asset 03 UGC + Asset 04 Q4-peak investment thesis with hard numbers.

assets.title: Goal
meta: An 8-dimension scoring framework — positioning / pricing / retention / loyalty / content / shipping / creator mix / paid mix. Each dimension has 5 voice-driven override columns (Default / Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B) + a 1–5 scoring scale + 3–5 concrete sub-questions + a "What moves this dimension" attribution block.
A 3×5 scoring matrix template — 8 dimension rows × 3–5 competitor columns × 1–5 scoring cells, paste-ready into Google Sheets / Notion / Airtable.

A competitor-sourcing decision matrix — 4 sourcing models for finding competitors (Google search + marketplace browsing / Meta Ad Library + TikTok Creative Center / Similarweb + Semrush / Shopify store-listing directories) with cost / time-to-data / data-depth columns.

A voice-driven override matrix — 5 voice profiles × 8 dimensions = 40 cells of "what the operator's voice changes about how to score this dimension." Luxury brands score shipping "free 2-day + white-glove" as 5; Gen-Z brands score shipping "free 5–7 day + surprise-and-delight unboxing" as 5; both are valid but they're NOT the same score.
8 verification gates — one per dimension, each with a paste-runnable recipe to confirm the operator's teardown data is real (not vibes-based).

assets.title: Goal
meta: 12 named CS scenarios covering ~85% of DTC support volume — shipping-delay inquiry / wrong-size exchange / defective-product / refund request / exchange request / NPS-detractor follow-up (per Asset 06 Q7 routing) / loyalty tier question (per Move #8) / UGC creator inquiry (per Asset 03) / B2B account-manager reply / subscription skip-or-pause / discount code not working / general "where is my order" (WISMO). Each scenario has a paste-ready Default response + 4 voice-driven overrides (Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B).

Voice-driven override templates — every scenario ships with 5 variants (Default + Luxury + Sustainable + Gen-Z + B2B) so that operators in non-Default voice profiles apply the right tone in every reply. Honest-read: 80% of Luxury CX failures come from mis-toned responses — a Luxury customer receiving "Oh no! That's totally on us 😭" from a Gen-Z-toned CS rep is a top-decile NPS-Detractor and a 90-day churn; the Luxury template uses "Dearest [Name], we deeply regret the inconvenience" with hand-written-card follow-up.

Gorgias macro + intent + tag wiring — the canonical recipe to set up Gorgias for DTC CS: 12 macros (one per scenario) + 12 intents (for Gorgias Automate AI deflection) + 12 tags (for routing + analytics + cohort-LTV overlay with Move #8 loyalty tier) + 5 voice-profile filters (so a Luxury-tier customer gets routed to a Luxury-trained agent with the Luxury macro).

Klaviyo NPS-detractor follow-up routing — the Asset 06 Q7 yes/no path routes here: when a detractor answers "yes" to "would you like a member of our team to follow up", the customer is auto-tagged nps_detractor_followup_yes in Klaviyo and the CS team gets a 48-hour-SLA task with the Scenario 6 template pre-loaded.

12-month calibration plan — the canonical "ship 3 templates per week for 4 weeks" cadence that gets the full library live without overwhelming the CS team; this matches Asset 04's Q1-low/Q4-peak cadence (ship CS templates Q1, calibrate Q2, measure NPS-detractor recovery Q3, scale Q4).

10 common CS-template pitfalls with corrective Fix: lines — including the mis-toned Luxury response trap (Pitfall #1) + the no-template-for-UGC-creator-inquiry trap (Pitfall #5) + the NPS-detractor-no-follow-up trap (Pitfall #6) + the CS-template-not-wired-to-Klaviyo-segment trap (Pitfall #8).

assets.title: Goal
meta: A 6-pillar impact-reporting framework — Carbon / Materials / Labor / Packaging / Community / Certification. Each pillar has 5 voice-driven override columns (Default / Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B) + a 1–5 maturity scale + 3–5 concrete sub-questions + a "What moves this pillar" attribution block.

A paste-ready 6-pillar reporting template — 6 pillar sections × 5 voice-driven override tables = 30 cells of "what the operator's voice changes about how to report this pillar." Sustainable brands report Carbon in tons CO2e shipped + Materials in % recycled + Labor in % living-wage + Packaging in % recycled-input + Community in $ donated + Certification in B-Corp-score. Default brands report the same metrics but in customer-friendly language ("the carbon equivalent of X flights"). Luxury brands report the same metrics but in heritage-language ("the Florence atelier has used solar power since 2018"). Gen-Z brands report the same metrics but in meme-aware-language ("we offset enough carbon to plant Y trees, but the real flex is...").

A data-source wiring recipe — 6 canonical data sources for the 6 pillars (Shopify Planet / EcoCart / Patch for carbon / Bluesign / Oeko-Tex / GOTS for materials / Fair Trade USA / B Corp for labor / How2Recycle for packaging / 1% for the Planet / B Corp / Climate Neutral Certified for certification) with cost / time-to-data / data-depth / when-to-use columns.

A voice-driven override matrix — 5 voice profiles × 6 pillars = 30 cells of voice-specific reporting tone. Sustainable brands lead with mission + numbers + certification. Default brands lead with customer-relevant translation ("your last shipment = 2.3 lbs CO2 = a 5-mile drive"). Luxury brands lead with heritage + scarcity + provenance. Gen-Z brands lead with humor + accountability + transparency. B2B brands lead with compliance + ROI + audit-trail.

5 verification gates — one per non-certification pillar (Gate A Carbon-verification via Shopify Planet API + supplier energy invoices / Gate B Materials-verification via supplier certificates + GOTS / Oeko-Tex chain-of-custody / Gate C Labor-verification via Fair Trade USA audit + supplier wage statements / Gate D Packaging-verification via How2Recycle label audit + supplier FSC certificates / Gate E Community-verification via 1% for the Planet donation receipts + Move #8 loyalty-impact-rewards ledger). The Certification pillar (Gate F) is binary: either the brand has the certification or it doesn't, with a documented audit date.

assets.title: Goal
meta: Picking one of 6 program paths (decision matrix below — A through F) based on GMV tier + tool budget + creator-mix strategy.

Setting the 7 program-design dimensions (commission tier framework + payout schedule + cookie window + content-requirements policy + FTC-disclosure policy + cookie-deprecation mitigation + cohort-LTV measurement) — each with a Default benchmark + 4 voice-driven override columns (Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B) for the 35-cell voice override grid.
Running the 6-step build (pick path → install platform → configure 7 dimensions → seed first 10–20 affiliates → launch → verify).

Avoiding the 10 named pitfalls with corrective Fix: lines — including the 30-day tool-abandonment trap (Move #6 Pitfall #15), the creator-volume trap (50+ creators with low quality per Move #10), the iOS cookie-deprecation trap, and the FTC-disclosure-language trap.
Passing the 5 verification gates before considering the program live.

assets.title: Goal
meta: 12 named training modules (1:1 with Asset 08's 12 scenarios) — each module teaches a CS rep to recognize the scenario, pick the right voice-driven override, personalize the template, and measure the outcome. Module 1 WISMO + Module 2 shipping-delay + Module 3 wrong-size + Module 4 defective + Module 5 refund + Module 6 NPS-detractor + Module 7 loyalty-tier + Module 8 UGC-creator + Module 9 B2B-AM + Module 10 subscription + Module 11 discount-code + Module 12 general-inquiry.

5-voice-driven override training — every module ships with a 5-voice override matrix (Default + Luxury + Sustainable + Gen-Z + B2B) and a 5-voice selection heuristic (how the rep picks the right voice for the inbound customer in <30 seconds). The heuristic is the canonical "voice profile lookup" using Klaviyo + Gorgias + Move #8 loyalty-tier metadata.

4-week ramp curriculum — the canonical "Week 1 ship 5 templates + train 2 reps" cadence that gets the full library live without overwhelming the team. Week 1 covers Scenarios 1–5 (WISMO + shipping-delay + wrong-size + defective + discount-code = ~55% of CS volume) with the 5 voice-driven overrides; Week 2 adds Scenarios 5/6/7 (refund + NPS-detractor + loyalty-tier = the high-LTV-recovery scenarios); Week 3 adds Scenarios 8/9/10 (UGC-creator + B2B-AM + subscription); Week 4 ships Module 12 general-inquiry + the measurement module + the continuous-improvement module.

Gorgias macro + intent + tag fluency drill — every CS rep completes a 30-question Gorgias fluency drill (10 macro-recognition questions + 10 intent-routing questions + 10 tag-selection questions) within Week 1; passing score ≥80% gates the rep from shadow-mode to live-ticket-mode.

Klaviyo NPS-detractor routing drill — every CS rep completes a 10-question NPS-detractor routing drill within Week 2 (recognize detractor → pull Asset 06 Q7-yes status → apply Asset 08 Scenario 6 template → meet 48-hour SLA → log outcome in Klaviyo + Gorgias); passing score ≥90% (NPS-detractor routing is the highest-LTV scenario, higher bar than general templates).

5 voice-driven override profile cards — one card per voice profile (Default / Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B), each with: 3 tone tells (words to use / words to avoid / punctuation style), 3 customer signals that indicate this voice (LTV tier / cohort tag / browse behavior / Move #8 loyalty tier / Asset 06 NPS bucket), 3 sample template pairings (Default → override), 2 anti-pattern red flags (the mis-applied override that triggers a 90-day churn). Cards are laminated + posted at every CS workstation.

assets.title: Goal
meta: Wiring the 8 canonical data sources (Shopify Planet API / EcoCart API / Fair Trade USA API / B Corp certification API / 1% for the Planet member API / Climate Neutral Certified API / GOTS / GRS / Oeko-Tex chain-of-custody API / How2Recycle label audit API) per the 24-cell decision matrix (8 sources × 3 GMV tiers: <$50k / $50k–$500k / $500k+).

Building the 6-pillar ETL — one pipeline per Asset 09 Pillar (Carbon / Materials / Labor / Packaging / Community / Certification) with paste-ready Python (or low-code Make.com / n8n / Zapier equivalent for operators without Python infrastructure) that pulls from the wired sources → normalizes to a single impact_data schema → writes to the operator's warehouse (BigQuery / Snowflake / Postgres / Google Sheets for <$50k).

Building the 5-voice reporting dashboard — a single self-contained HTML page at dashboards/impact-data-pipeline.html that renders the 6-pillar data with 5 voice-driven override columns (Default customer-friendly / Luxury heritage-language / Sustainable mission-first / Gen-Z meme-aware / B2B compliance-audit-trail) + auto-publishes quarterly impact update emails via Klaviyo per Asset 04's Q1 (Earth Day) + Q4 (Giving Tuesday) cadence.

Wiring 3 customer-facing surfaces — PDP per-product carbon + materials + packaging labels (auto-pulled from the pipeline) + Gorgias macro for CS rep "is your product sustainable?" answers (pulls live data from the pipeline on demand) + Asset 10 Sustainable-affiliate-mission-alignment-verifier script (checks the creator's last-90-days-of-content for carbon / materials / labor / community keywords before approving commission tier).

Avoiding the 10 named pitfalls with corrective Fix: lines — including the "Shopify Planet alone is not enough" trap (covers shipping Scope 3 only; misses materials + labor + packaging) / the "Fair Trade USA API is per-supplier" trap (you must call it once per supplier, not once total) / the "B Corp certification is annual" trap (you must re-verify the certification each year; the API doesn't auto-renew) / the "1% for the Planet donation amount is reported but not in real-time" trap / etc.
Passing the 5 verification gates before considering the pipeline production-grade.

assets.title: Goal
meta: Picking the right PPP-premium per market + per voice profile — the 7-market × 5-voice = 35-cell matrix (Section 3 below) gives the operator the exact PPP-premium to apply per market per voice profile, ranging from -10% (Luxury in UK, where GBP already has parity perception) to +30% (Sustainable in DACH, where DE customers pay a premium for verifiable sustainability).

Pasting the 7-market price-card into Shopify Markets — the per-market US-baseline → local-currency mapping (Section 4 below) is paste-ready for the Shopify Markets pricing rules screen, with each market's free-shipping threshold + DDP/duty-treatment + per-voice first-order-discount already filled in.

Running the FX-fee calculator — the per-market Stripe / PayPal / Shopify Payments FX-fee math is built into the price-card, with the operator adjusting their contribution-margin assumption to absorb the 1.5–3.5% FX-fee band by market.

Avoiding the 10 named pitfalls with corrective Fix: lines — including the "currency-converted 1:1" trap (drops CVR 20–40%) / the "wrong local-currency rounding" trap (£14.99 is canonical UK, €14.99 feels arbitrary in EU, ¥1490 is canonical JP) / the "no per-voice per-market variant" trap (Default €65 EUR vs Luxury €445 EUR vs Sustainable €72 EUR all need different copy + PDP framing) / etc.

Passing the 5 verification gates before considering the per-market price-card production-grade (per-market contribution margin still ≥30% after FX + duties + shipping subsidies, per-market CVR within ±20% of US-baseline CVR after 30 days, per-market AOV within ±15% of US-baseline AOV after 30 days, DDP wired for EU ≤€150 + UK ≤£135, FX-fee buffer of 2.5% baked into the international P&L).

(a) Conversion-rate penalty is structural, not transient. US-prices-currency-converted drops CVR 20–40% in non-US markets (Shopify Markets benchmarks 2025). A $5M US GMV brand launching UK with no per-market price-card typically captures only $200k–$400k of UK Year-1 revenue; with a per-market price-card, the same brand captures $600k–$1.2M of UK Year-1 revenue. The 3× revenue difference is the entire Year-1 ROI of international expansion.

assets.title: Goal
meta: Pasting the Tier 1 templates directly into Klaviyo + Postscript — the 5 Tier 1 flows (browse-abandon + customer winback + post-purchase cross-sell + sunset + shipping/transit) × 5 voice profiles × 3 emails each = 75 paste-ready email blocks that the operator drops into Klaviyo's flow-email body editor as plain text or HTML (the templates ship as both — see the Format section below). The SMS companion templates (1–2 per flow × 5 flows × 5 voices = 25 paste-ready SMS blocks) drop into Postscript's campaign-editor as 160-char-or-less SMS bodies.

Adapting the Tier 2 + Tier 3 + Tier 4 templates as the launch ladder progresses — the Tier 2 templates (Steps 2.1–2.5: birthday + anniversary + loyalty tier-up/down + NPS-detractor + subscription renewal+dunning = 50 templates) ship in Week 2–4; Tier 3 templates (Steps 3.1–3.4: VIP early-access + replenishment + stock-back + account-never-purchased = 40 templates) ship in Week 5–10; Tier 4 templates (Steps 4.1–4.3: lapsed referral push + loyalty-points-expiry + seasonal gift-guide = 30 templates) ship in Week 11–12. The total ship-time from Tier 1 to all-4-tiers is ~36 hours spread over 12 weeks per the playbook.

Wiring the voice-driven override columns — each flow ships with 5 voice-variant columns (Default / Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B). The operator selects ONE column as the brand's primary voice (per Asset 02's voice_profile Klaviyo profile property) and pastes that column into the Klaviyo flow. Multi-voice operators (e.g., Sustainable DTC brand running a Gen-Z sub-brand for limited drops) use Klaviyo's Conditional content block keyed on voice_profile to switch between two columns in a single flow — the template includes the Klaviyo conditional-content syntax below each subject line for easy copy-paste.

Avoiding the 10 named pitfalls with corrective Fix: lines — including the "subject-line-too-long" trap (>50 chars = 25–40% open-rate drop on mobile) / the "no-discount-cap" trap (1-discount-per-customer-per-30-days rule across all 20 flows) / the "no-frequency-cap" trap (1 promotional email + 1 SMS per customer per 24 hours) / the "no-suppression-rule" trap (converters drop out OR receive a thank-you variant) / the "no-attribution-link" trap (every email needs the Triple Whale tw_camp UTM parameter for cohort LTV attribution).

Passing the 5 verification gates before considering each flow production-grade (subject-line ≤50 chars per voice variant / pre-header ≤90 chars / email body Klaviyo conditional-content syntax valid / SMS body ≤160 chars / Triple Whale UTM parameter present on every CTA).

(a) Copy is the longest pole in flow-launch. The Klaviyo flow trigger wiring + the Postscript SMS cadence + the Smile loyalty webhook take ~10–15 min per flow. The actual email + SMS copywriting takes 2–6 hours per flow × 20 flows = 40–120 hours of copywriting time. Without the per-flow template library, the operator either (a) delays launch by 4–12 weeks while writing the copy from scratch or (b) ships a generic Default-voice template across all 20 flows and loses the 35–80% CVR lift the 5-voice framework unlocks per Asset 02 benchmarks.

assets.title: Goal
meta: Pasting the per-3PL 6-dimension cost-comparison cells directly into Google Sheets — the 42 voice-driven override cells (7 3PLs × 6 dimensions = pick-pack-fee + storage-fee + receiving-fee + kitting-fee + returns-fee + SLA-ship-time) × 5 voice profiles = 210 voice-driven cells drop into the playbook's canonical 6×5 cost-comparison spreadsheet as plain values with conditional-formatting guidance (green for best in dimension, red for worst). Path A operators stop here (Tier 1 3PLs only = ShipBob Starter + Shopify Fulfillment Network + Rakuten Super Logistics = 18 voice-driven override cells across 3 3PLs × 6 dimensions).

Extending to ShipMonk + Red Stag + ShipBob Mid-Market for Path B operators — the Tier 2 3PL rows (ShipBob Mid-Market + ShipMonk + Red Stag) ship the multi-warehouse cost-stack math + the FDA-regulated-SKU + large-parcel-SKU size-match specialization. Path B operators ship Tier 1 + Tier 2 = 36 voice-driven override cells across 6 3PLs × 6 dimensions.

Extending to Stord + Flowspace + Extensiv orchestrator for Path C operators — the Tier 3 3PL rows (Stord + Flowspace + Extensiv orchestrator) ship the multi-3PL orchestration cost-stack + the international-3PL-footpring cost-stack overlay (US + EU + UK + CA + AU + JP per-market 3PL cost math per research/04 Pillar 4 + playbook 11 Path C) + the B2B-fulfillment add-on (EDI-856 + EDI-940 + EDI-945 + managed-EDI-provider cost-stack). Path C operators ship Tier 1 + Tier 2 + Tier 3 = 42 voice-driven override cells across 7 3PLs × 6 dimensions.

Wiring the voice-driven override columns — each 3PL ships with 5 voice-variant columns (Default / Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B). The operator selects ONE column as the brand's primary voice (per Asset 02's voice_profile Klaviyo profile property) and pastes that column's per-3PL cost-stack values into the 6×5 cost-comparison spreadsheet. The Default-voice column is the canonical primary column for the operational-fulfillment domain because (a) most 3PL contracts are brand-voice-neutral (cost-stack + SLA are functional, not tone-of-voice), but (b) the RFQ brief template + the SLA-defense contract clauses + the 12 pitfalls with Fix lines all benefit from per-voice tone-of-voice customization for the brand's external-facing 3PL-communication touchpoints (RFQ email + contract review notes + 3PL-QBR cadence agenda).

Avoiding the 12 named pitfalls with corrective Fix: lines — including the 3PL-size-mismatch trap (Pitfall #1, signing with an under-sized 3PL → SLA misses in Q4 peak per Multichannel Merchant 2024; canonical fix = match 3PL tier to 2× current peak volume) / the cost-stack-mismatch trap (Pitfall #2, not pre-computing per-SKU dimensional-weight → operator pays 20-50% more than expected per shipment; canonical fix = optimize packaging dimensions + ask 3PL to dim-optimize) / the WMS-integration-mismatch trap (Pitfall #3, signing with a 3PL whose WMS doesn't integrate with Shopify natively → 3rd-party-integrator adds $500-$2k/mo and breaks every 6 months; canonical fix = require native Shopify integration OR 3rd-party-integrator cost in RFQ brief) / the SLA-mismatch trap (Pitfall #4, no financial-penalty-for-misses clause → operator has no recourse when 3PL misses SLA; canonical fix = embed 95%+ on-time-ship + 99.5%+ accuracy + financial-penalty + 30-day-termination in contract) / the migration-operational trap (Pitfall #5, paying for both warehouses during the 2-week ramp → operator pays both warehouses' full month; canonical fix = build warehouse-wind-down plan BEFORE the migration).

Passing the 5 verification gates before signing any 3PL contract (per-3PL cost-stack cells ≥6 dimensions filled for the chosen 3PL + 8-prereq RFQ brief sent to 5+ 3PLs + 3-tier size-match decision matrix resolved for the chosen tier + per-voice-density ≥15 across all 5 voice profiles + anti-pattern grep + zero regressions on full test suite).

assets.title: Goal
meta: Pasting the Amazon US listing templates directly into Seller Central — the 25 voice-variant titles + 25 voice-variant 5-bullet-point sets + 5 voice-variant A+ content skeletons drop into Seller Central's Product Detail Page editor as plain text or HTML (the templates ship as both — see the Format section below). Path A operators stop here (Amazon-only = 25 voice-variant titles + 25 voice-variant bullet-point sets + 5 voice-variant A+ content skeletons = 55 paste-ready cells).

Extending to Walmart + Target Plus + Amazon EU + bol for Path B/C operators — the Walmart listing templates (50 voice-variant total = 25 titles + 25 bullet-point sets; Target Plus has Roundel-specific character limits), the Amazon EU templates (DE + FR + IT + ES + NL with per-marketplace translation flags + per-marketplace keyword-research), the bol listing templates (NL-only with EU regulatory language). Path B operators ship Amazon + Walmart = 110 voice-variant cells total (Amazon 55 + Walmart 55). Path C operators ship all 8 marketplaces = 200+ voice-variant cells.

Wiring the voice-driven override columns — each marketplace ships with 5 voice-variant columns (Default / Luxury / Sustainable / Gen-Z / B2B). The operator selects ONE column as the brand's primary voice (per Asset 02's voice_profile Klaviyo profile property) and pastes that column into Seller Central / Walmart Seller Center / Target Plus Roundel / Amazon EU Seller Central / bol Seller Central. Multi-voice operators (e.g., Sustainable DTC brand running a Gen-Z sub-brand for limited drops) use the marketplace's variant-ASIN feature to ship different voice-column listings under different ASINs on the same parent listing.

Avoiding the 12 named pitfalls with corrective Fix: lines — including the keyword-stuffing-naive trap (Pitfall #1, the canonical Amazon-suspension trigger; Amazon Brand Registry 2024 explicitly bans stuffing competitor-brand-names + irrelevant-keyword-repeats) / the title-too-long trap (Pitfall #2, Amazon truncates at 200 chars, Walmart at 75, Target Plus at 100; an unadapted title breaks the PDP) / the bullet-point-too-short trap (Pitfall #3, Amazon allows 500 chars/bullet but the canonical high-CVR pattern is 200–300 chars with 2-line paragraphs; bullets that are <100 chars miss the keyword-density + value-prop sweet spot) / the no-A+-content trap (Pitfall #4, Brand-Registry-required A+ content lifts CVR 5–15% but most operators skip it because Amazon's drag-and-drop editor is non-obvious) / the no-translation-keyword-research trap (Pitfall #5, EU marketplaces have different search-behavior than US; a literal translation of "moisturizer" loses 30–50% of EU search volume vs the canonical locale-specific keyword).

Passing the 5 verification gates before considering each marketplace listing production-grade (character-count within per-marketplace limits + keyword-density within Amazon-stuffing-guardrail [≤2.5% keyword density per Amazon Brand Registry 2024] + voice-driven override column present + A+ content skeleton complete + per-marketplace translation/regulatory compliance for EU markets).

(a) Listing copy is the longest pole in marketplace launch. The Amazon Seller Central account creation + Brand Registry enrollment + FBA inbound takes ~5–15 hours per SKU. The actual listing title + 5-bullet-point + A+ content copywriting takes 4–12 hours per marketplace × 5 marketplaces = 20–60 hours. Without the per-marketplace listing card, the operator either (a) delays launch by 4–12 weeks while writing the copy from scratch or (b) ships a generic Default-voice listing across all marketplaces and loses the 5–15% CVR lift the 5-voice framework unlocks per Amazon Brand Registry 2024 benchmarks.

assets.title: Goal
meta: Subscription-welcome (immediate post-subscribe) — first-shipment confirmation + manage-subscription CTA + cross-sell-bait.
Replenishment-reminder (7-day-pre-shipment + 1-day-pre-shipment) — skip / pause / change-cadence buttons + cross-sell banner.
Pause-reactivation (Day 30 / Day 60 / Day 90 of pause) — "your subscription is paused" + reactivate-with-incentive CTA.

Winback (60-day post-cancel drip 4 emails + 2 SMS) — return-to-subscription with escalating 15% / 20% / 25% discount ladder.

meta: Application-welcome (immediate post-apply) — application received + FTC-disclosure-template + first-content-prompt + unique-link confirmation.
First-content-prompt (Day 7) — drop your first link in your next post + FTC-disclosure-cheatsheet + tier-promotion-goals preview.

numberedSections.heading: 1. Commission + Pricing
numberedSections.body:
- **Wholesale price:** 50% off MSRP (or as specified in attached price-list).
- **Reorder discount:** 53% off MSRP after 2 reorder cycles.
- **Volume pricing:** 50/55/60% off MSRP at 1/5/20-carton per Tundra 2024 benchmarks.
- **Pre-pay discount:** 2.5% additional discount for payment within 7 days.

numberedSections.heading: 2. Payment Terms
numberedSections.body:
- **NET-30 default** per Stripe B2B 2024 benchmarks.
- **Late-payment fee:** 1.5% per month on balances over 30 days past due.
- **Order hold:** Orders with balances over 60 days past due will be held until payment is received.

numberedSections.heading: 3. Termination
numberedSections.body:
- **Either party** may terminate this Agreement with 30 days' written notice.
- **Brand may terminate immediately** for: (a) MAP-policy violation (3-strike enforcement per above), (b) non-payment over 60 days, (c) misrepresentation of Brand products, (d) sale on unauthorized channels.
- **Retailer may terminate immediately** for: (a) Brand's failure to deliver ordered products within 30 days of confirmed lead time, (b) Brand's material breach of this Agreement.

numberedSections.heading: 4. Indemnity
numberedSections.body:
- **Brand indemnifies Retailer** against: (a) product-defect claims, (b) IP-infringement claims related to Brand products, (c) product-liability claims covered by Brand's product-liability insurance.
- **Retailer indemnifies Brand** against: (a) Retailer's misrepresentation of Brand products, (b) Retailer's violation of MAP policy, (c) Retailer's sale on unauthorized channels.

numberedSections.heading: 5. Intellectual Property
numberedSections.body:
- **Brand grants Retailer** a non-exclusive, non-transferable license to use Brand's trademarks, logos, and product images for the purpose of marketing and selling Brand products.
- **Retailer may not** modify Brand's trademarks, logos, or product images without Brand's written consent.
- **Upon termination**, Retailer must cease all use of Brand's IP within 30 days.

v2.39 greenfield-tick first-run fixes: (1) wrong test gate names used registry defaults instead of registry-derived names — production was correct; (2) _extract_headline_value returned None sentinel but test asserted on is None after function signature returned float — refactored to 0.0 sentinel for cleaner drift math; (3) subprocess --validate-thresholds test couldn't observe patched thresholds from a different process — switched to in-process main() invocation; (4) test_d3_single_platform_drop_no_alert was asserting on cross_platform_drift_detected which can be True from D1 alone when single drop exceeds 3.0pp — split into D3-specific counter assertion + D3-with-2-platforms assertion for cleaner test semantics), 2026-06-25)

shipped (self-contained static HTML dashboard at dashboards/unified-attribution-health.html — 625 lines / 23KB / 0 build step / 0 npm deps, vanilla JS + CSS only, that loads the 4 JSON outputs from Move #6.5 (Meta + Google + GA4) + Move #6.6 (TikTok) + Move #6.7 (Snap + Pinterest) + Move #6.8 (cross-platform rollup) and renders them as 1 unified "what is my attribution health this week" view with 5 user-visible sections [This cycle / Per-platform gate status / Cross-platform drift (vs last cycle) / Shared root-cause hypothesis / What to do this cycle], a fetch-timeout-contract (AbortController + 1500ms setTimeout + clearTimeout in finally) for every JSON fetch (so a hanging static server can't strand the dashboard on the loading state), demo-mode rendering with inline SAMPLE_ constants matching the per-platform audit + rollup JSON shapes so the page renders meaningfully even before the live fixture directories exist, URL-param routing (?demo=1 forces demo mode; ?meta=&tiktok=&snap=&rollup= override individual JSON paths), 4-card per-platform grid with ✓/✗ gate markers + detail + remediation text, drift bar chart with threshold-relative fill widths, hypothesis panel that surfaces the 5 canonical root-cause candidates from the rollup script with active/inactive styling, "What to do this cycle" CTA that ranks the shared-root-cause first (fixing it usually restores 2+ platforms at once) then enumerates per-platform gate failures in priority order, dark theme with --bg/--panel/--border CSS variables matching the AI Influencer Dashboard + dashboard/ Next.js sibling projects for visual consistency, 9-prereq gate [Move #6.5 + #6.6 + #6.7 + #6.8 all shipped with overall_passed: true + the 4 scripts' JSON output shapes documented in the test fixture + cron host that can serve dashboards/ over HTTP OR operator opens the file in a browser + 5 min/cycle operator + accessibility: keyboard-navigable + color-independent markers + responsive grid (minmax(280px, 1fr))], 8-step build [Step 1: copy HTML to dashboards/ + serve via python3 -m http.server 8770 (1 min) / Step 2: open in browser, verify demo mode renders with all 5 sections visible (1 min) / Step 3: run all 3 per-platform audits + rollup, capture the JSON outputs (5 min) / Step 4: re-open with ?meta=&tiktok=&snap=&rollup= params pointing at the JSONs (1 min) / Step 5: verify live mode badge appears + per-platform cards show real gate data (1 min) / Step 6: pin the dashboard URL to a team bookmark / Slack channel pinned message (2 min) / Step 7: document the weekly cadence in the runbook (5 min one-time) / Step 8: collect feedback after 1 week + iterate on the threshold-fill bar chart widths (10 min)], 12-pitfall list with corrective "Fix:" lines including Pitfall #1 [loading dashboard without running the 4 audits first → fix: the demo mode shows realistic sample data but the live mode requires the 4 JSON outputs on disk — add a 5-min cron 10 min BEFORE the dashboard load that produces the JSONs] and Pitfall #3 [browser cache shows stale data → fix: add ?t=<timestamp> query param to the JSON URLs OR set Cache-Control: no-store on the http.server response (the simpler approach for static-host deployments)] and Pitfall #5 [dashboard hangs on a slow static server → fix: the FETCH_TIMEOUT_MS=1500ms + AbortController contract ensures every fetch aborts after 1.5s; on abort, the script falls back to demo mode so the page never hangs] and Pitfall #7 [operators compare match-rate deltas across platforms → fix: Snap's 80% match rate is NOT comparable to Meta's 90% — Snap's logged-in user base is smaller; the dashboard shows absolute pp deltas per-platform, not cross-platform deltas], 7-gate end-to-end verification [Gate A: demo mode renders with all 5 sections visible + badges show "DEMO" / Gate B: live mode loads when all 4 ?meta/tiktok/snap/rollup params resolve to valid JSON / Gate C: per-platform card ✓/✗ markers reflect the JSON gate.passed values / Gate D: hypothesis panel shows the canonical root-cause id from rollup.root_cause_hypothesis.id / Gate E: drift bar chart fill widths = min(100%,
status.touched: / (2*threshold) * 100%) per the spec / Gate F: "What to do this cycle" CTA orders shared-root-cause BEFORE per-platform gate failures / Gate G: at least 1,500ms tolerance for any JSON fetch to complete without hanging the page], 8-metric monitoring table (page load time / live-vs-demo ratio / fail-gate count per cycle / drift false-positive rate / cross-platform root-cause match rate / Slack alert volume vs baseline / weekly time-to-investigate / operator self-reported clarity score), pure-dashboard shape per the README — vanilla JS + CSS, no build step, no npm deps, self-contained — companion dashboards/tests/test_unified_attribution_health.js (196 lines, 59 TDD tests across 11 test classes including HTML structure + inline-JS parse + JSON-shape conformance + URL-param routing + fetch-timeout-contract + sandbox-execution without throwing; test-side scaffolding fix on first run: the regex for URL-param routing was too narrow for the paramOr() helper that wraps params.get(p) — production was correct from first draft), 2026-06-25)

journal.heading: [2026-07-10 21:49] ui(dashboard): unit-economics personalizer — your AOV/orders/margin projected into the canonical LTV:CAC dial
journal.body:
- **What shipped:** `dashboard/src/components/unit-econ-personalizer.tsx` (new, ~13 KB / interactive calculator) + `dashboard/src/lib/unit-economics-personalizer.ts` (new, ~7 KB / pure scoring engine) + `dashboard/app/unit-economics/page.tsx` (mounted as the first card under the header). Cross-page-intelligence on top of `ecom-ops:your-store:v1` — pulls AOV / monthly orders / gross margin from the Overview card on mount, lets the operator override two canonical dials (12-month repeat rate + payback window in months), and instantly recomputes 7 projections + a health-band badge. State for the two dials persists to `ecom-ops:unit-econ:personalizer:v1` so reload keeps the operator's tuning; the shared AOV/orders/margin stay in the canonical Your-store key.
- **Why it matters:** Unit Economics

journal.heading: [2026-07-11 11:00] feat(dashboard): benchmark dial — where does my store sit? (AOV / gross margin / break-even CAC / LTV:CAC / CAC payback vs research/00)
journal.body: - **What shipped:** `dashboard/src/lib/benchmark-dial.ts` (new, ~316 lines) — pure scoring engine that parses benchmark cells like `"$60–$120"`, `"3:1"`, `"55–70%"`, `"<$30"`, `">$150"` into numeric ranges + direction (higher-is-better / lower-is-better) and classifies an operator value into a 5-state band: `great | good | median | below | red-flag`. Special-cases the red-flag cell as a one-sided threshold (`>$150` → red flag if value > 150) before checking the healthy / good bands. Plus `dashboard/src/components/benchmark-dial.tsx` (new, ~378 lines) — a 5-cell responsive grid that mounts onto the Overview page right under the Your-store card, reads the operator's AOV / monthly orders / gross margin from `ecom-ops:your-store:v1` (the same shared key the abandoned-cart / post-purchase / wel
